abbreviations.$version: Support operator: ==, <, >, <=, >=, ~= and ^=. Three digit only, e.g., 1.3.1. '~=' allows patch version change, which means 1.3.x, while '^=' allows minor version change, which means 1.x.x. Blank space is allowed, e.g., '<= 1.3.0'

note: Rules are applied in defined order. Upper rule applies first
It's actually better to update base profile to the latest version, then add rules to ensure backward compatibility, instead of the other way around

rule.$explanations: For version between 1.9.0 and 1.10.0, rename the key. This can also be written as '$v': '~=1.9.0'
